target.name: Agility Robotics
target.founder: Jonathan Hurst, Damion Shelton, Mikhail Jones

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $5.2B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.3B.

private.name: Exotec
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series D), post-money $2B

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Agility Robotics is a Series D robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $400M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable, including same-sector companies at Series C through Series E stages: Divergent ($2.3B, Series E), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B, Series C).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy for enterprise value ($5.2B), applies a typical 1.5x step-up multiplier from Series D to the next stage, yielding $7.8B. Peer-median analysis across the four sampled comparables produces a median valuation of $1.8B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $2.3B. The peer-based method reflects observed valuations at the same stage and adjacent stages within robotics and manufacturing.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.5B (low) to $9.0B (high), with a median of $4.8B. This wide dispersion reflects inherent tension between capital-raised momentum (stage-progression method) and market-traded comparables (peer-median method). Key uncertainties include path-to-profitability, addressable market realization, and whether Agility commands a premium or discount relative to the peer set. The peer-median estimate ($1.8B) is more conservative and grounded in observed transactions; the stage-progression estimate ($7.8B) assumes steeper upside momentum typical of growth-stage venture funding.
